display.purpose: Cockpit readouts — race countdown and big stat values. Tight −1.5px tracking.

shadows.name: fab-glow
shadows.purpose: Yellow bloom under the floating log FAB — the one place accent light leaks.

shadows.name: toast-lift
shadows.purpose: Transient confirmation toasts floating above the panel.

shadows.name: spotlight-scrim
shadows.purpose: Tutorial spotlight — full-screen dark scrim punched by the highlighted element.

motion.name: view-fade
motion.purpose: View entrance: opacity + 4px translateY. Honor prefers-reduced-motion with instant swap.

motion.name: spotlight-glide
motion.purpose: Tutorial spotlight and card moving between steps (ease-out-quint feel).

breakpoints.name: app-column
breakpoints.purpose: The app is a single centered phone-width column; content never exceeds 520px. No multi-column responsive breakpoints — it is phone-first by design.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single loudest element per screen — the primary action.

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Default for anything that isn't THE action — graphite with a hairline border.

components.name: Chip (multi-select)
components.description: Pill toggle for equipment/filters; selected state flips to yellow with a leading check.

components.name: Card
components.description: Flat panel-graphite container with a hairline border. No shadow, never nested.
components.html:
Pacing calculator

Turn a goal finish time into run and station targets.

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Raised-graphite field with an uppercase micro-label; focus shifts the border to dim gold.

components.name: Status Pill
components.description: Tiny uppercase phase/status marker; tinted background of its own hue.
components.html: basebuilddone ✓

components.name: Bottom Nav + FAB
components.description: Four tabs flanking the central floating yellow log button; active tab is voltage yellow.

components.name: Cockpit Hero
components.description: The Home centerpiece — race countdown in Display type over a graphite gradient with a corner yellow bloom and a weeks-to-race progress bar.
components.html:
Race countdown
84 days

narrative.overview: ROXREADY is a dark instrument panel worn on the wrist of a training day. The surface is near-black; a single high-voltage yellow is the live readout — the one number, the one action, the one thing that matters right now. Everything else recedes into graphite so the yellow never has to compete. It holds a deliberate tension: clinical precision (honest splits, exact loads, fade percentages that don't flatter) that earns the right to a gritty, motivating voice. Density is phone-native and single-column, glanceable one-handed, mid-workout, in bright gym light. Depth comes from tonal layering, not shadow theatrics. It rejects the cluttered fitness app, childish gamification, and the generic SaaS dashboard.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black graphite base with a single high-voltage yellow accent (#ffd60a)
One system font family across four weights — no display/body pairing
Tonal layering for depth; shadows reserved for the two floating elements (FAB, toast)
Big tap targets, high contrast, fast entry — built for the gym floor
Semantic status color (green/amber/blue) only where meaning demands it

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Voltage Yellow marks exactly one thing per screen: the primary action or the live metric. Two yellow fills competing in one viewport is forbidden — demote one to secondary graphite.

rules.name: The Faint-Ink Ceiling
rules.body: #5c5f68 (Faint Ink) is decorative only — timestamps, dividers, disabled hints. Any text a user must read to act uses Dim Ink (#9a9da6) or brighter.

rules.name: The 16px Floor Rule
rules.body: Body copy never drops below 16px, and inputs stay at 16px to stop iOS zoom-on-focus. Captions are for metadata only.

rules.name: The Weight-Not-Face Rule
rules.body: Emphasis comes from weight (700/800) and the yellow, never from a second font.

rules.name: The Flat-Panel Rule
rules.body: Cards and containers cast no shadow. Separation comes from a tonal step and a hairline border. Shadows are reserved for elements that literally float (FAB, toast, scrim).

dos: Do keep Voltage Yellow to one element per screen — the primary action or the live metric.
Do build depth with tonal graphite steps (#0e0f11 → #17181c → #1e2025) and 1px #2a2c33 borders.
Do keep body text ≥16px and ≥4.5:1 contrast; use Dim Ink (#9a9da6) or brighter for anything readable.
Do carry meaning in one system font's weights (700/800) and the yellow, not in a second typeface.
Do keep tap targets chunky (≥44px) — used mid-workout, one-handed, in bright light.
Do state the honest number, then let the gritty voice motivate.
donts: Don't put readable text in Faint Ink (#5c5f68) — it fails AA (~2.9:1). Timestamps and dividers only.
Don't use gradient text or background-clip:text — the yellow is a solid fill or metric color, never a gradient on glyphs.
Don't nest cards, or default to a card when a tonal step or hairline divider would do.
Don't add ad rails, stacked banners, or competing CTAs — one clear next action per screen.
Don't add cartoon mascots, confetti storms, badge spam, or guilt-trip streak mechanics.
Don't introduce a blue-accent, hero-metric-template dashboard look.
Don't shadow flat surfaces; shadows are only for the FAB, the toast, and the tutorial scrim.
Don't run press states hotter than a subtle scale/tone — the intent is precise & minimal, not theatrical.
